Oh my gosh, I thought we were done. There's another meeting. Yes Quarterly meetings. What is the quarterly meeting? Okay So at the meeting this meeting it will be the beginning of each quarter And what is the purpose of this quarterly meeting? It is to remind the team of the vision culture and values review the company progress from the prior quarter and Determine next quarter priorities. Okay, same exact as the monthly except you're looking at a larger span of time So I would like to give you an example that we also provided in the portal What's the purpose of this to intense fine remind our team of the culture and values and create a 30-day goal, right? time okay, this is going to be the same day and time each quarter When you're thinking about that think okay, it's gonna be the Friday of the end of every quarter at 9 a.m. Okay What's the length of this so if you're starting on the smaller team? Say 1 to 5 I would say start with minimum a half day and this could go all the way up into a full day, okay? Who's going to attend? This is going to be determined by the size of your team and your gut and so you will make mistakes You might not invite all the right people the first time or second time or third. I've done it It's okay. Not the end of the world. Just do the quarterly meeting. Okay, if you're a small team Maybe the whole team right so you have under five people I would say include all of them because then it just doesn't make sense if there's like three of you in there They have to relay the information to two people. That's stupid If it's a larger team you want key managers and stakeholders so people who are involved in decision-making right? What's the agenda going to look like okay first is you're gonna review the agenda and your desired outcome just like in the monthly From there you want to share your backstory your why why you started the gym? What were your struggles? Why are we doing this? Okay, as well as? You're going to go into reviewing your client's district mission You're going to review your core tenants how they tie into the gym and then review your yearly goals How repetitive is this? Oh my gosh? I feel like it's the same thing as the monthly it is You need to keep repeating these things You think that you're repeating things you think that you are saying the same thing over and over your team hasn't heard for the five times you've said it they're thinking about their boyfriend They're gonna go to see after work. They're thinking about that client that annoyed the crap out of them They're thinking about how they want to go do something else and they're tired or hungry. Okay? That's why you repeat yourself so much because people are distracted Then you're going to review the five core functions, which I will talk about next From there review your scoreboard of last quarter What was your goal and where did you actually fall and then discussing that with your team saying hey guys? Where do you think we succeeded and where did we fall short and why let's dig into it? You know where did you where can we each take ownership and where we might not have met a goal? Versus where can we all take ownership in sharing the wins? Okay? and then establishing new quarterly goals, so You will have questions that new team members may have or may ask them okay again What upcoming commitments do we have in the next year based on your legals? What opportunities do we have or should we create to get closer to our yearly goals? Right what is feasible to get done in the next quarter to move closer to that goal? Okay, is it an internal play is a new campaign is it a referral program? You know what is it that you're going to do to get close to that critical number of the apts? Okay, let your team contribute their ideas Let them speak and be comfortable with the silence if they're not comfortable to speak yet Okay, the first three to four times we ran these I felt like I was the only one talking and I felt like nobody else was contributing their ideas it took about ten months until About four times of doing this and then finally people came with their own ideas They started contributing and now I just sit back and listen to the team So I'm telling you this is not something that's gonna happen overnight You have to train people and consistently over and over and over again give them trust in space Give them trust in space give them trust in space Okay, the trust meaning you trust them to input their ideas and the space to speak those ideas meaning silence, okay? Then you solidify all the goals So you have them all one of you is the one taking notes during this meeting, right? And then as you are wrapping up you solidify and you reiterate them you then assign ownership in deadlines Okay, so for quarterly you're gonna break it down by typically it's gonna be broken down to 30 days 90 days 60 days as well as weeks, right? And then after that you're going to recap and close You want to send this out to everyone that attended the meeting you want them to print it out And you want everyone to have it on hand at all times
